title: Executive Order 12548—Grazing Fees

flags.justification: Grazing fees are set by a precise mathematical formula with objectively computed inputs (Forage Value Index, Beef Cattle Price Index, Prices Paid Index) and a defined floor and cap, leaving essentially no room for discretionary judgment.
The formula is entirely mechanical, leaving no ambiguity about how the fee is computed or who is responsible.
Single purpose: establishing the grazing fee formula.
An exact formula with exact weightings, an explicit annual-change cap (plus or minus 25 percent), and an explicit floor ($1.35 per animal unit month).
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing fee-setting mechanism implementing the Public Rangelands Improvement Act's own formula structure, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 3 explicitly continues existing rules, practices, policies, and regulations rather than removing any oversight.

Because the fee is formula-driven from publicly available Statistical Reporting Service data, any affected party can independently verify the calculation — a genuine, structural transparency mechanism.
A routine, narrow fee-setting mechanism; no broad authority claim.
Agriculture and Interior jointly exercise their own existing respective authorities without conflict.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. A genuinely exemplary order for its type: an entirely objective, publicly-verifiable mathematical formula leaves essentially no room for arbitrary exercise of the fee-setting authority.
